text.data-narration: Inheritance is a mechanism that allows a child class to inherit the properties and behavior of a parent class.
There are two types of inheritance in Java: Single Inheritance and Multiple Inheritance. Java does not support multiple inheritance.
Single Inheritance: A child class inherits from a single parent class.
Multiple Inheritance: A child class inherits from multiple parent classes. Java does not support multiple inheritance.
The super keyword is used to access the members of the parent class from the child class.
Upcasting: When a child class is assigned to a parent class reference variable, the super keyword is used to access the members of the parent class.
Downcasting: When a parent class reference variable is assigned to a child class reference variable, the super keyword is used to access the members of the child class.

circle.data-narration: Inheritance is a fundamental concept in Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) that allows one class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class.
Inheritance is achieved using the extends keyword in Java.
The super keyword is used to access the members of the parent class from the child class.

line.data-narration: Single Inheritance: A child class inherits from a single parent class.
Multiple Inheritance: A child class inherits from multiple parent classes. Java does not support multiple inheritance.
Upcasting: When a child class is assigned to a parent class reference variable, the super keyword is used to access the members of the parent class.
Downcasting: When a parent class reference variable is assigned to a child class reference variable, the super keyword is used to access the members of the child class.
